title: soil-rs-correlation-screening: code and reproducible outputs for the leakage-controlled screening of 512 remote-sensing features against agrochemical soil properties (Northern Kazakhstan steppe)

description:
Code and reproducible outputs for the correlation-screening study "Digital Soil Mapping of the Steppe Zone of Northern Kazakhstan: Relationships Between Agrochemical Soil Properties and Multimodal Remote Sensing Data". Spearman screening of 512 multimodal remote-sensing features against six agrochemical soil properties (pH, SOC, NO3, P2O5, K2O, S) under leakage control (temporal alignment + out-of-farm Farm-LOFO), with Benjamini-Hochberg FDR, nested variance components, spatial-autocorrelation and confounding analyses, and an empirical feature taxonomy.

This archive contains the analysis code (math_statistics/, ML/) and the reproducible result tables and figures. All numbers and figures are deterministic (SEED = 42) and byte-reproducible from the underlying dataset as described in the README.

The raw soil laboratory data are NOT included: they are commercially sensitive and are archived separately (see Related identifiers). The companion machine/deep-learning modelling paper is doi:10.3390/agriculture16111239.
notes: Raw sample-level soil laboratory data (master_dataset_old.csv, soil_analysis.xlsx) are deliberately excluded from this archive because they are commercially sensitive; the analysis is fully reproducible from that private dataset per the README. TODO for authors before publishing: (1) confirm which of the two related Zenodo records is the dataset vs. the companion code and refine the relation types if needed; (2) add ORCID iDs to creators.
